briefs.title: Council to Vote on: Accepting Donated Lease Rights for Craneway Pavilion, Richmond CA
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on accepting a donation of leasehold interests — meaning the rights to occupy and use the property under an existing lease — for the Craneway Pavilion waterfront site from Orton Entertainment, LLC. The proposed agreement would also transfer related personal property and an $80,000 cash contribution to the City, with escrow (a third-party-held transfer process) set to close May 1, 2026. However, a third party, PB Development Group, has filed a lawsuit claiming over $10 million in damages related to operations at the site and is urging Council to delay approval.
briefs.action_required: If your business has ties to the Craneway Pavilion site, attend the Council meeting and seek legal advice before May 1.

key_dates.label: Craneway Pavilion leasehold donation escrow closing deadline

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Richmond Open Session for Public Comment Before Closed Meeting
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to hold an open session at 3:30 p.m. on April 21, 2026, to hear public comment before moving into a closed session — a private meeting not open to the public. This is an opportunity for residents and business owners to speak to Council before confidential matters are discussed.
briefs.action_required: Attend the April 21, 2026 open session at 3:30 p.m. to share your comments with Richmond Council.

key_dates.label: Richmond Council open public comment session before closed meeting
